summary: Camera pause-mode decomp/refactor work centered on src/melee/cm/camera.c and cm/types.h. The PR renamed the camera movement struct to CameraTransformState, propagated transform/transform_copy naming through camera APIs, identified several pause-camera fields such as pause_eye_offset, pause_eye_distance, pause_up, min_distance, and max_distance, and implemented pause zoom/rotation-related functions. decomp.dev reported one new full match, Camera_8002BAA8, and improvements to Camera_8002BD88 and Camera_8002A768. There was no PR body or human review feedback in the slice.
what_changed: Renamed struct CameraMovement to CameraTransformState and updated forward declarations and camera function signatures that pass this state, including Camera_80029AAC, Camera_80029BC4, Camera_80029C88, Camera_8002A768, Camera_8002AF68, and Camera_8002B1F8.
Renamed Camera fields movement and movement_lerp to transform and transform_copy. transform_copy was annotated as running the same tween logic but not obviously used for output.

Converted many unknown Camera fields from unk_* names to offset-style x* or array-style _* names, while giving semantic names to pause-camera fields: min_distance, max_distance, pause_eye_offset, pause_eye_distance, and pause_up.

Implemented Camera_8002BAA8 with a comment identifying it as Camera_PauseZoom. The implementation computes the pause eye offset length, clamps pause_eye_distance between min_distance and max_distance, normalizes the offset, and stores the scaled offset back.

Implemented Camera_8002BC78 and helper logic for camera basis clamping/orthonormalization. It clamps near-vertical forward vectors, preserves pause_up when clamped, and rebuilds right/up with cross products and normalization.

Implemented Camera_8002BD88 with a comment identifying it as Camera_PauseRotate. It derives forward/up/right vectors from pause_eye_offset, clamps vertical rotation near poles, updates pause_up, applies y and x rotation offsets, normalizes, scales by pause_eye_distance, and writes back pause_eye_offset.
Added a small vec_len(Vec3*) helper returning sqrtf(x*x + y*y + z*z).
Renamed static camera direction constants in camera.static.h, including cm_803B73B8 to cm_WorldForward and cm_803B73D0 to cm_WorldUp, and made these static Vec3 const.
Updated camera.h prototypes to use CameraTransformState and changed Camera_8002BD88 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void Camera_8002BD88(f32, f32).
Added explanatory comments in grstadium.c mapping Pokémon Stadium state values to default, fire, grass, water, and rock.
smart_moves: The PR separated semantic naming from unknown offset naming: fields with understood pause-camera meaning received names like pause_eye_offset and pause_eye_distance, while uncertain fields were converted to x-offset style rather than overinterpreted.

Renaming CameraMovement to CameraTransformState better reflects that the struct contains interest, target_interest, position, target_position, fov, and target_fov rather than just movement parameters.
Factoring vector length into vec_len and basis reconstruction into an inline OrthonormalizeBasis made the pause zoom/rotate code easier to reason about while preserving matching progress.
The pause rotation code explicitly handles near-pole forward vectors by clamping y to +/-1 and zeroing incompatible rotation input, which documents a likely source of edge-case behavior.
World direction constants were given semantic names and const qualification, making call sites like Camera_8002A768 and Camera_8002BD88 clearer without changing data layout.

The author left uncertainty visible in comments, including transform_copy usage and a TODO/fake constant note in Camera_8002BD88, which helps future decompilers avoid treating provisional logic as fully understood.
decomp_lessons: When a struct mostly transports eye/interest/fov target and current values, naming it around transform state can clarify many call sites at once and reduce misleading parameter names like movement.
For large globals like Camera, use semantic names only where behavior is supported by code evidence; otherwise x-offset and _array naming keeps layout searchable and honest.
Pause camera work benefits from identifying the basis vectors explicitly: forward from negative eye offset, up from stored pause_up or world up, and right from cross products.
Matching a function may require replacing decompiler-style reciprocal-square-root expansion with a clearer helper or sqrtf expression if the compiler emits the desired sequence.
Header prototypes should be updated together with implementation parameter type renames so later PRs do not keep propagating stale typedefs.
Small stage comments, such as mapping numeric Stadium variants to fire/grass/water/rock/default, can improve future decomp context even when the code behavior is unchanged.
naming_conventions: Unknown scalar/bitfield names were normalized to x-offset style such as x2BC, x2C0, x398_b0, x399_b6, x39A_b7.
Unknown arrays were normalized to leading-underscore offset style such as _8C, _B0, and _1B0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: decomp.dev reported Camera_8002BAA8 improved from 87.07% to 100.00%, adding a new 61-byte full match in main/melee/cm/camera.
decomp.dev reported Camera_8002BD88 improved from 0.00% to 85.80%, a +556 byte unmatched-function improvement.
decomp.dev reported Camera_8002A768 improved from 65.62% to 66.21%, a +12 byte improvement, alongside the rename from raw cm_803B73B8 accesses to cm_WorldForward.
Camera_8002BAA8 replaced a decompiler-looking manual __frsqrte length calculation with vec_len/sqrtf plus normalization and distance clamping.
Camera_8002BC78 and Camera_8002BD88 use PSVECCrossProduct and lbVector_Normalize to reconstruct an orthonormal camera basis.
Camera_8002BD88 contains an explicit TODO noting that the x-axis path used a fake constant expression, so that function was not fully solved despite the large match improvement.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only issue comment was the decomp.dev report showing matched code increased by 464 bytes overall, with one new match and two unmatched-function improvements.
searchable_terms: Pokemon Stadium fire grass water rock
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that finish Camera_8002BD88 or remove the TODO/fake constant in the x rotation path.
Search whether transform_copy is later proven to affect rendering or can be given a better semantic name.
Search for later renames of x2BC, x2C0, x341_b3_b4, and x35C after more pause-camera behavior is understood.
Check whether Camera_8002C010 and nearby pause camera functions were decompiled after this PR.
